channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: UNLV senior offensive lineman Ben Christman dies
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — UNLV senior offensive lineman Ben Christman, who transferred after last season from Kentucky, has died, the university announced. Christman, who was 21, was found dead in an off-campus apartment on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tigers expect RHP Alex Cobb to miss start of season with hip injury after signing $15M, 1-year deal
item.description: LAKELAND, Fla. (AP) — The Detroit Tigers are expecting Alex Cobb to miss the start of the season. The team's injury report at the start of spring training on Wednesday included Cobb, who had an injection last week to treat hip inflammation that developed as the right-hander was throwing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lindsey Vonn says she 'would be proud to race with anyone' in team combined at the Olympics
item.description: SAALBACH-HINTERGLEMM, Austria (AP) — It’s not just downhill and super-G that Lindsey Vonn wants to compete in at the age of 41 at next year’s Milan-Cortina Olympics.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DAMPF, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Former coach Vilda denies pressure on Hermoso to downplay kiss after Women's World Cup final
item.description: MADRID (AP) — Jorge Vilda, the former coach of Spain women’s soccer team, told a judge on Wednesday that ex-soccer federation president Luis Rubiales did not ask him to put pressure on Jenni Hermoso following the outcry that erupted after Rubiales kissed the player.
item.dc:creator: TALES AZZONI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New Pirates 1B Spencer Horwitz to miss at least 6-8 weeks following wrist surgery
item.description: New Pittsburgh Pirates first baseman Spencer Horwitz is expected to miss most of spring training after undergoing surgery on his right wrist.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Soccer stampede: High-scoring Leigh girls should be force in playoffs
item.description: North Carolina commit Maggie Currie has scored 16 goals for Leigh this season: 'When I’m on the field with her, I feel like I’m watching someone play in a video game.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: Max Homa is trying to dig himself out of another hole
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — On the list of U.S. Open final qualifying sites announced this week is Valencia Country Club in the Southern California city where Max Homa was raised. That might be an option for him, which would have seemed absurd a year ago.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Wrexham says one of its players hospitalized after road incident on way home from match
item.description: WREXHAM, Wales (AP) — Wrexham midfielder Elliot Lee was taken to the hospital after being involved in what the Welsh club described as a “road traffic incident” on the way home from a match, the team said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Eagles' roster overhaul between two Super Bowl titles is an NFL rarity
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Seven years after Doug Pederson and Nick Foles delivered Philadelphia its first Super Bowl title, Nick Sirianni and Jalen Hurts did it again with a 40-22 win over Kansas City in Super Bowl 59.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bellingham strikes late for Real Madrid in 3-2 win at Man City in Champions League 1st leg playoff
item.description: MANCHESTER, England (AP) — Real Madrid’s Galacticos just don’t know when they are beaten. Manchester City just keep on making the same mistakes. Jude Bellingham’s stoppage-time goal sealed a stunning comeback for Madrid and a 3-2 lead in its Champions League playoff against City.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UConn's Liam McNeeley draws inspiration for 38-point performance from Creighton fans heckling Hurley
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — UConn freshman star Liam McNeeley hadn't played in front of a crowd as hostile as the one the Huskies faced in Omaha on Tuesday night. He found the environment inspiring.
item.dc:creator: ERIC OLSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: De Beque's Burke Largent scores 1,000th point as Dragon fans go crazy at Caprock
item.description: As Largent eclipsed the 1K mark on a tough bucket over three defenders, the scores of royal blue in the Caprock stands went absolutely berserk.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Top-ranked Auburn snaps back with an 80-68 win over Vanderbilt
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Denver Jones scored 21 points as No. 1 Auburn held off Vanderbilt 80-68 on Tuesday night to avoid the Tigers' first losing skid of the season.
item.dc:creator: TERESA M. WALKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NASCAR drivers divided on new rule that could guarantee Helio Castroneves a spot in the Daytona 500
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — A controversial new NASCAR rule designed for “world-class drivers" could give Helio Castroneves an automatic berth into the Daytona 500, and, should the four-time Indianapolis 500 winner use the provisional, it would make for the largest field in a decade.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles family featured in Super Bowl commercial
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) – Taco Bell marked its return to the Super Bowl with a commercial that featured nearly 400 fans, including a couple from Los Angeles and their newborn baby.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: UPenn could face civil rights violation after trans athlete ban
item.description: It follows President Donald Trump's executive order banning transgender women from competing in women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: A presidential first: Trump at the Super Bowl, latest chapter in a complicated legacy with football
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — As a student, Donald Trump played high school football. As a business baron, he owned a team in an upstart rival to the NFL and then sued the established league. As president, he denigrated pros who took a knee during the national anthem as part of a social justice movement.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Even NFL referees get jersey numbers
item.description: You likely aren't looking for the jersey of your favorite NFL referee — so why do they have jersey numbers?
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Eagles beat Chiefs 40-22 in lopsided Super Bowl LIX
item.description: The Kansas City Chiefs wanted to make history with three back-to-back-to-back championships, but Super Bowl LIX was dominated by the Philadelphia Eagles, who won the game 40-22.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Utah HC gives up season-high 7 goals to Carolina
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (ABC4 Sports) - After two overtime thrilling victories, the Utah Hockey Club gave up a season-high seven goals in a 7-3 loss Saturday to the Carolina Hurricanes. Seth Jarvis had two goals and two assists, and Sebastian Aho had two goals and an assist for the Hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: House Divided: Wife an Eagles fan, husband cheers for Chiefs in NOLA
item.description: When one of the couple grows up in Kansas a Chiefs fan, and the other in South Jersey an Eagles fan, one would expect the marriage to be tested but Jaime and Dave Dailey make it fun.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, Fox 5

item.title: Tiger Woods will play in Genesis Invitational at Torrey Pines
item.description: Tiger Woods, the host of The Genesis Invitational and an 82-time PGA Tour winner, will play at Torrey Pines Golf Course next week.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Public visitation scheduled for Virginia Halas McCaskey
item.description: Virginia Halas McCaskey, the 102-year-old matriarch of the Chicago Bears, will be remembered on Tuesday, Feb. 11, at an event held by Oehler Funeral Home in Des Plaines.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Blackhawks take losing streak into matchup with the Predators
item.description: Nashville Predators (18-27-7, in the Central Division) vs. Chicago Blackhawks (16-31-6, in the Central Division) Chicago; Friday, 8:30 p.m. EST BOTTOM LINE: The Chicago Blackhawks play the Nashville Predators after losing three straight games.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Big Game Bound: Breaking down the matchups headed into Super Bowl weekend
item.description: The Chiefs and Eagles will kickoff Super Bowl LIX in two days from the Caesars Superdome in New Orleans, both with hopes of being crowned the kings of the NFL.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, NewsNation

item.title: Sports reporter dies while in New Orleans for Super Bowl LIX
item.description: Adan Manzano died on Wednesday in New Orleans while on assignment to cover the Super Bowl for both Telemundo Kansas City and Tico Sports.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Jason Kelce and look-alike posse take on New Orleans
item.description: Five of the most passionate NFL fan duos in America were challenged to be the first team to find the real Jason Kelce.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Penguins at the Birch Aquarium make their Super Bowl predictions
item.description: Some penguins at the Birch Aquarium at University of California, San Diego's Scripps Institution of Oceanography made their bets on which team will win the big game on Super Bowl Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Report: Bulls, Ball agree to contract extension
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago Bulls and point guard Lonzo Ball have agreed to a two-year contract extension, according to ESPN's Shams Charania. Charania reports the deal is worth $20 million over two years.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Rams headed Down Under for NFL's 1st game in Australia
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) — The Los Angeles Rams are set to make NFL history Down Under next year. The squad announced Wednesday that they're headed to Melbourne, Australia, in 2026 to play the league's first regular season game in the country.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5
